BEER VOLTA.rule: - **Shop Rule (BEER VOLTA)**: The core format is `Japanese Name / English Name [Optional Size/Info]`. The product name may include suffixes like bottle/can size (e.g., '500ml', '355ml'), shipping info (e.g., '【空輸】'), arrival dates (e.g., '≪12/20-21入荷予定≫'), or other markers that should be noted but not part of the core beer/brewery name. The English part typically starts with the brewery name followed by the beer name. Extract brewery and beer names from the English portion when available.

ちょうせいや.rule: - **Shop Rule (ちょうせいや)**: The STRICT format is `【Beer Name / Brewery Name】`. IMPORTANT: The content BEFORE the slash (/) is ALWAYS the Beer Name, and the content AFTER the slash is ALWAYS the Brewery Name. Prefixes outside 【】 (e.g., discount rates '30%OFF', 'ポイント10倍', '在庫整理', '入荷予定') and suffixes (e.g., '(賞味期限/2025.11.15)') must be IGNORED. For collab breweries with × or ×, include all brewery names (e.g., '京都醸造×Inkhorn' → 'Kyoto Brewing x Inkhorn Brewing'). Assume all products are "beer" unless explicitly stated otherwise.

一期一会～る.rule: - **Shop Rule (一期一会～る)**: The name format varies but commonly includes both Japanese and English brewery/beer names. Patterns include: `Brewery (JP) Beer (JP) (Brewery EN Beer EN)`, or abbreviated versions. There may also be special editions or collaboration markers. Extract brewery and beer names from the clearest available portion (typically English when present). Watch for parenthetical English translations.

アローム.rule: - **Shop Rule (アローム)**: The format is typically `Japanese Brewery / Japanese Beer Name (Beer Style) [English Brewery / English Beer Name]`. The first part before the slash is the brewery (Japanese), the second part is the beer name (Japanese), often followed by style in parentheses. The English version in square brackets provides the definitive brewery and beer names. Prioritize extracting from the English portion in square brackets when available.
